laws.title: Public Law 38-262 | Fund the Government Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: Full text is present. The act is not self-repealing and includes both temporary appropriations and a permanent repeal/rewrite of prior schedule rules, so it remains operative rather than expired.

provisions.text_summary: Short title only.
provisions.notes: Keep as a concise historical note.

provisions.text_summary: Definitions for department, appropriations, and emergency fund.
provisions.notes: No exact Code placement is stated.

provisions.text_summary: Budget allocations and emergency fund appropriations.
provisions.notes: Appropriations do not belong in permanent Code text.

provisions.text_summary: Oversight and accountability for fund misuse.
provisions.notes: Administrative direction only.

provisions.text_summary: General schedule pay scale table.
provisions.notes: If later integrated, this should be mapped to federal pay law, but the source does not identify a precise Code target.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals section 6 of PL 32-230 and assigns schedule authority to OPM.
provisions.exact_change: Repeal section 6 of PL 32-230; vest schedule-defining authority in the Office of Personnel Management / human capital officer.
provisions.notes: This is a real repeal instruction to the prior law.

provisions.text_summary: Immediate enactment / effective date.
provisions.notes: No separate Code amendment.

current_implementation.detail: Current XML carries a note at t40/s101/4, but it still contains a full quoted-content dump and an Archive record link. The branch note prose differs from origin/main only in boilerplate cleanup; it still should not be a full-text note block.

recommended_actions: remove the quotedContent dump and Trello link from rp-pl038262-codification.
keep only a concise statutory note for the appropriations and repeal history.
if the pay-scale provision is meant to alter permanent salary law, codify it textually in the appropriate Title 5 provision instead of leaving it in note form.

laws.title: Public Law 38-263 | National Security Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: The act creates an ongoing national-security eligibility framework, classification rules, and administrative-subpoena safeguards; it also repeals prior CRACK Act titles I-IV, but nothing in the source self-expires.

provisions.text_summary: Short title.
Findings and purposes.
Definitions for covered position, protected information, and related terms.
provisions.notes: No precise U.S. Code insertion target is stated.

provisions.text_summary: Severability.
Repeals CRACK Act titles I-IV; retains titles V-VI until this act takes effect.
provisions.exact_change: Repeal titles I-IV of the CRACK Act of 2022; preserve titles V-VI only until the effective date of this act.
provisions.notes: Direct repeal of prior-law titles.

provisions.text_summary: National Security Council governance and records preservation.
provisions.notes: The source references Title 50 but does not give a clean strike/insert target.

provisions.text_summary: Eligibility determinations, notice, response, review, disclosure limits, and implementation safeguards.
provisions.notes: No exact Code section is identified for insertion.

provisions.text_summary: Classification limits and in-camera / ex parte procedure safeguards.
Administrative subpoenas and security-sensitive licensing protections.

current_implementation.detail: The note sits at the title-50 anchor, which is directionally sensible, but it still embeds a full quoted-content dump and Archive record link. The live note is still note-only, not integrated code text.

recommended_actions: replace the full-text dump with a concise statutory note at the title-50 anchor.
remove the Trello link.
if later codification is desired, split the act into actual Title 50 amendments instead of leaving the whole framework in note form.

laws.title: Public Law 38-264 | Organized Crime and Racketeering Modernization Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: The act repeals CRACK Act titles V and VI and installs a continuing racketeering framework with penalties, civil remedies, and disqualification rules; it is not self-limiting or expired.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, findings/purposes, definitions, severability.
Repeals CRACK Act titles V and VI, supplies savings, supersession, and immediate effective date.
provisions.exact_change: Repeal titles V and VI of the CRACK Act of 2022 and preserve only the stated savings and effective-date rules.
provisions.notes: Direct repeal of prior-law titles.

provisions.text_summary: Prohibited racketeering conduct and attempt/conspiracy rule.
provisions.notes: No precise Code target is given.

provisions.text_summary: Criminal penalties, forfeiture, and procedural protections.
Civil remedies and no private damages action.
Public-trust disqualification rules.
Venue and expedited proceedings.

current_implementation.detail: The current note is in the title-18 anchor, but it still carries a full quoted-content dump and Archive record link. The branch differs from origin/main only in note wording, not in the underlying note-only approach.

recommended_actions: remove the quotedContent dump and Trello link from rp-pl038264-codification.
keep the repeal history and racketeering framework as a concise statutory note unless and until a precise Title 18 placement is identified.
mark the CRACK Act title repeal in the prior-law history trail.

laws.title: Public Law 38-265 | Employment Clarity and Federal Workforce Modernization Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: The act expressly repeals earlier employment statutes and installs a continuing private- and federal-employment framework with review, anti-retaliation, and enforcement rules.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, findings/purposes, and definitions.
provisions.evidence: "Employment Clarity and Federal Workforce Modernization Act of 2026"

provisions.text_summary: Repeals FERA24, the Intelligence Community Employment Act, and the redundant separation framework; includes non-revival and supersession rules.
provisions.exact_change: Repeal the named prior laws to the extent stated and preserve only the non-revival / supersession rules.
provisions.notes: Multiple prior-law repeals are explicit.

provisions.text_summary: Severability and immediate effective date.
Private-sector at-will default, discrimination/retaliation limits, and notice rules.
provisions.notes: No exact U.S. Code insertion target is stated.

provisions.text_summary: Federal service categories, adverse-action process, provisional service, at-will agreements, emergency suspension, internal review, and judicial review.
Intelligence-community at-will exceptions and security-clearance access limits.
Dual-employment and conflict-of-interest restrictions with enforcement.
Bureau enforcement, private remedies, anti-retaliation, and limitation periods.

current_implementation.detail: The note is anchored in Title 5, which is the right subject area, but it still carries a full quoted-content dump and an Archive record link. The branch note text is also a little cleaner than origin/main, but the implementation remains note-only.

recommended_actions: remove the full-text dump and Trello link.
retain a concise statutory note at a Title 5 employment anchor.
if the repository later codifies this framework, move the direct amendments into actual Title 5 and related-title text.

laws.title: Public Law 38-266 | Speedy Trial Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: The source creates continuing speedy-trial rules and directly amends Public Law 4-32; nothing in the enactment self-terminates.

provisions.text_summary: Short title.
Repeals prior speedy-trial provisions in PL 22-162 and the prior chaptered framework.
provisions.target: Pub. L. 22-162 / 18 U.S. Code Chapter 208 Part II
provisions.exact_change: Repeal the named prior speedy-trial provisions in full.
provisions.notes: This is a genuine repeal instruction.

provisions.text_summary: Trial timing rules, sanctions, and filing/response deadlines.
provisions.notes: The source does not specify a precise Code section to strike and replace.

provisions.text_summary: Amends Public Law 4-32 section IV(b)(iii).
provisions.exact_change: Replace the arrest-warrant execution text with the five-day nonappearance / Discord-or-ROBLOX-PM language.
provisions.evidence: "Section IV(b)(iii) of Public Law 4-32 shall be amended"

provisions.text_summary: Amends Public Law 4-32 section B(b)(iii).
provisions.exact_change: Replace the bench-warrant execution text with the five-day nonappearance / Discord-or-ROBLOX-PM language.
provisions.evidence: "Section B(b)(iii) of Public Law 4-32 shall be amended"

provisions.text_summary: Severability and effective date.

current_implementation.detail: The current note is only a note block at the title-18 anchor, but SEC. 6 contains a determinable amendment to the prior Public Law 4-32 text. The branch retains the note-only wrapper instead of integrating the warranted textual amendment.

recommended_actions: apply the SEC. 6 amendment text to the PL 4-32 history chain or the proper codified home of that enactment.
replace the current note-only block with a concise history note.
remove the Trello link and the full-text dump.

laws.title: Public Law 39-267 | Reform Sentencing Act
laws.status_basis: The source repeals PL 34-251 and replaces it with ongoing sentencing rules, enforcement, and multi-charge limits; it is not self-limiting or sunsetted.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, purpose, severability, and definitions.
Repeals Public Law 34-251 in its entirety.
provisions.exact_change: Repeal PL 34-251 in full.

provisions.text_summary: Felony sentencing reform.
provisions.notes: The source does not identify a precise codified strike/insert target.

provisions.text_summary: Misdemeanor sentencing reform.
Enforcement by law-enforcement officers.
Multi-charge sentencing cap.
provisions.evidence: "shall not be jailed for more than thirty (30) minutes"

current_implementation.detail: The live entry is still a note block at the title-18 anchor, but the source is an actual sentencing rewrite with a direct repeal of PL 34-251. The current branch keeps the note-only approach and does not integrate any real code text.

recommended_actions: remove the full-text dump and Trello link from the current note.
keep the repeal history for PL 34-251 as a concise note.
if the sentencing rules are meant to be permanent Code text, assign a precise Title 18 placement and codify them textually.

laws.title: Public Law 40-268 | Big Beautiful Budget Act
laws.status_basis: This is a one-off appropriations act with short-form enactment language and no permanent statutory target; the appropriations and oversight provisions are time-bound rather than enduring Code text.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and definitions.
Budget allocations and emergency fund appropriations.
provisions.notes: This should not be permanent Code text.

provisions.text_summary: Relocation of funds to the Treasury.
Oversight and accountability.
Enactment / effective date.

current_implementation.detail: The current note is a full-text dump at a Title 31 anchor, but the act is temporary appropriations legislation. The note should be reduced to a brief history entry or excluded from Code text entirely.

recommended_actions: exclude the appropriations and oversight provisions from permanent Code text.
replace the full dump with a brief historical note only if needed.
remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 41-269 | Federal Enhancement Against Foreign Intrusion Amendments Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: The act makes continuing amendments to Public Law 24-173 and adds a new disclosure/enforcement section; nothing in the source sunsets or self-repeals.

provisions.text_summary: Short title.
provisions.evidence: "Federal Enhancement Against Foreign Intrusion Amendments Act of 2026"

provisions.text_summary: Amends Section III of PL 24-173 to bar elected officials from foreign employment or formal affiliation and to preserve no automatic removal rule.
provisions.exact_change: Replace Section III of PL 24-173 with the foreign-affiliation prohibition and referral language.
provisions.evidence: "Section III of Public Law 24-173 is amended"

provisions.text_summary: Inserts a new disclosure and enforcement section after Section IV of PL 24-173.
provisions.exact_change: Insert a new Section V requiring disclosure of foreign affiliation and routing enforcement to 22 U.S.C. 618.

provisions.text_summary: Severability and immediate effective date.

current_implementation.detail: The current note lives at the title-50 anchor, but the source contains two determinable amendments to PL 24-173. The note-only wrapper is insufficient for the enacted amendment text.

recommended_actions: apply the PL 24-173 Section III replacement text and the new Section V insertion as actual amendment history.
replace the full note dump with a concise statutory note.
remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 41-270 | Washington Diplomacy Complex Corrections Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: The act directly revises the Washington Diplomacy Complex Act 2025 in multiple sections and adds technical corrections; it is an ongoing corrective amendment, not a repealed or expired law.

provisions.text_summary: Short title.
Amends Section 3 of the Washington Diplomacy Complex Act 2025 definitions.
provisions.exact_change: Replace the definitions section with the new definitions of Complex, Restricted Security Area, Department, and United Nations Secretariat.
provisions.evidence: "Section 3 ... is amended to read as follows"

provisions.text_summary: Amends Section 4 jurisdiction, security, and control provisions.
provisions.exact_change: Replace Section 4 with the expanded jurisdiction, restricted-area, and enforcement text.
provisions.evidence: "Section 4 ... is amended to read as follows"

provisions.text_summary: Amends Section 5 United Nations access provisions.
provisions.exact_change: Strike 'free and full access' and replace subsection (c) with the revised access-limitation text.

provisions.text_summary: Technical corrections and redesignation of Section 6.
provisions.exact_change: Redesignate Section 6 as implementing regulations and correct duplicate headings / numbering issues.

provisions.text_summary: Rule of construction and immediate effective date.
provisions.notes: The source contains duplicate SEC. 6 headings; preserve both concepts in history, not as separate Code text.

current_implementation.detail: The live entry is only a note block at the title-40 anchor, but the source gives precise replacement text for the underlying Washington Diplomacy Complex Act sections. The current note does not integrate the actual amendments.

recommended_actions: apply the section-by-section text amendments to the Washington Diplomacy Complex Act 2025 history chain.
replace the current full note with a concise statutory note and technical-correction history.
remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 41-271 | The Great Change Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: This is a broad integrating act that repeals, amends, transfers, and preserves multiple existing laws while installing continuing reforms across security, commerce, ethics, military service, and whistleblower enforcement.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, table of contents, definitions, construction, severability, and effective date.

Repeals the Washington Diplomacy Complex Boundary Act, terminates obsolete commission authorities, strips anti-supersession clauses, preserves valid final actions, corrects clerical defects, blocks revival, and repeals the Oath and Offices Protection Act.
provisions.target: WDCBA; National Commission on Judicial Activity, Efficiency and Accountability Act; OOP
provisions.exact_change: Repeal or terminate the named prior laws and preserve only the stated savings / no-revival rules.

provisions.text_summary: Removes automatic security clearances, amends the National Security Act of 2026, bars automatic SCI/SAP access, creates expedited review, oversight, anti-retaliation, and classification safeguards.
provisions.exact_change: Amend the cited national-security statutes to eliminate automatic clearances/access and to add the new oversight and retaliation protections.

provisions.text_summary: Commerce Dispersal Act amendments and function transfers to State and Justice.
provisions.exact_change: Amend the Commerce Dispersal Act sections on commerce, trade, intellectual property, enforcement, and copyright functions as enacted.

provisions.text_summary: Amendments to 18 U.S.C. 205, definitions, protected government legal work, and savings clauses.
provisions.exact_change: Replace and supplement the 18 U.S.C. 205 text as enacted.

provisions.text_summary: Commissioned-officer minimums, temporary waivers, command authority, and validity of actions during noncompliance.
provisions.exact_change: Amend the affected staffing laws to the new minimum, waiver, command, and validity rules.

provisions.text_summary: Whistleblower-protection amendments, retaliation rules, burden of proof, identity protection, noncompliance rules, command responsibility, and reporting.
provisions.exact_change: Insert the new whistleblower, retaliation, remedies, identity, oversight, and command-responsibility provisions into the prior whistleblower framework.

provisions.text_summary: Conforming amendments and successor-reference rules.

current_implementation.detail: The current entry is a note block, but the act is mostly a set of determinable textual amendments to existing laws. The live note also carries a full quoted-content dump and a Trello link, so it is not an acceptable final implementation.

recommended_actions: convert the direct amendments in Titles II-VI into actual text changes in the affected Code or prior-law sections.
trim the note block down to a concise history note for the residual uncodified material.
remove the Trello link and the full-text dump.
